Tech-debt: Reconstruire dist/ontowave.js depuis src/main.ts (migration API roots/i18n) (#48) La nouvelle librairie TypeScript (`src/main.ts`) n'avait jamais été compilée vers `dist/`. L'ancienne librairie monolithique (~550KB, API `locales`/`sources`) était toujours servie sur CDN, tandis que toutes les pages de démos utilisaient encore l'ancienne API incompatible. ## Changements ### Build / Distribution - Reconstruit `dist/ontowave.js` + `dist/ontowave.min.js` depuis `src/main.ts` via `vite build --config vite.config.dist.ts` (bundle IIFE, 4.5MB minifié) - Copie automatique vers `docs/ontowave.min.js` dans le script `build:package` - Ajout du script `build:lib` dans `package.json` ### Corrections de bugs dans la librairie - **`view.ts`** : lookup lazy de `#app` (évite crash si script chargé avant le DOM) - **`main.ts`** : redirection initiale utilise `i18n.default` du config (était hardcodé `#index.md`) - **`enhance.ts`** : vérification de délégation de sous-module requiert ≥3 segments de chemin (était ≥2, déclenchait des 404 sur toutes les routes) - **`search.ts`** : un index bundlé vide (`[]`) arrête le fallthrough vers le fetch du sitemap - **`main.ts`** : la fonction `check()` vérifie le bundle avant de faire un `fetch` (supprime les erreurs console 404 pour `/nav.yml` et `/search-index.json`) ### Migration des pages de démos (8 pages) Passage de l'ancienne API à la nouvelle sur toutes les pages `docs/demos/**/*.html` : ```html
``` ### Config & structure - Création de `public/config.json` et `docs/config.json` pour le site principal - Mise à jour de `index.html` (racine) vers entry Vite TypeScript (`src/main.ts`) - Mise à jour de `docs/index.html` vers la nouvelle API - Snapshots E2E supprimés (régénérés automatiquement au prochain run) ## Checklist - [ ] Convention de commit respectée - [ ] Lint OK / Type-check OK / Tests OK - [ ] Docs/README mis à jour si nécessaire - [ ] Breaking changes documentées ou aucune - [ ] Tests ajoutés ou N/A - [ ] Portée petite et réversible ## Notes - Les demos référencent `/ontowave.min.js` (local) pendant cette transition. Après publication npm, le CDN `@latest` sera à jour automatiquement. - Le bundle inclut toutes les dépendances (mermaid, katex, markdown-it, yaml) — 4.5MB minifié, 1.4MB gzip. feat(heureka): enables auth for heureka and false positive remediation UX and code improvements (#1535)

* chore(heureka): removes action column header and correct its layout

* chore(heureka): use pills to show image versions

* fix(heureka): preserve revert false positive message by avoiding inline component definitions

Inline component wrappers defined inside ImageIssuesList caused React to
unmount and remount RemediationHistoryPanel on every re-render, destroying
its local revertMessage state before it could be displayed. Replaced
VulnerabilitiesTab and RemediatedVulnerabilitiesTab wrappers with direct
JSX rendering of their content components.

* fix(heureka): only expose auth user ID when running in embedded mode

* fix(heureka): fix revert false positive UX in remediated vulnerabilities

- Inline tab content components to prevent RemediationHistoryPanel from
  unmounting on re-render, which was discarding the revert success/error message
- Remove empty action column from remediated vulnerabilities table

* refactor(heureka): extract useTimedState hook to consolidate auto-expiring message logic

* chore(heureka): improve success messages and add spacing in remediation panel

- Reword false positive and revert success messages to be more natural
- Note that changes may take a few moments to appear in the tables
- Add top margin between the status message and the data grid
- Add className prop support to ErrorBoundary

* chore(heureka): improve false positive UX and data freshness

- Show a spinner in the actions cell while marking a false positive or
  reverting a remediation, keeping the modal mounted so error feedback
  still works
- Replace throw-error pattern with return-value error signaling
  (Promise<{ error: string } | void>) so API errors are shown as
  messages in the UI instead of unhandled exceptions
- Add className support to ErrorBoundary for layout spacing
- Display success messages for 10 s with copy clarifying that status
  updates may take 5–6 minutes to appear in the tables
- Align hamburger menus to the end of their column via minContentColumns
- Prevent duplicate heavy API requests by setting staleTime: 2.5 min in
  fetchImages and fetchRemediations; TanStack Query deduplicates
  in-flight requests natively, and the cache window avoids redundant
  re-fetches on tab switches
- After a successful false positive or revert, poll for fresh data at
  +2.5 min and +5 min by invalidating the TanStack Query cache and
  triggering a re-render; pending polls are cancelled when a new
  operation starts or the component unmounts
- Rename issuesPromise to activeIssuesPromise for clarity

* fix(heureka): align fallback and loading row colSpan with DataGrid column count in remediated vulnerabilities table

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(heureka): store shouldExpire predicate in a ref to prevent timer reset on re-renders

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(heureka): fix useTimedState timer reset by storing shouldExpire predicate in a ref

* chore(heureka): adds changeset

* fix(heureka): hide revert spinner as soon as success message appears in remediation history panel

* fix(heureka): hide revert spinner as soon as success message appears in remediation history panel

* fix(heureka): show success message immediately after API call and run data refresh in background

* fix(heureka): address Copilot review comments on PR

- Add isMountedRef guard to prevent setRefreshKey after unmount in
  background poll callbacks; scope invalidateQueries predicate to
  current service/image instead of all queries
- Clear stale revertMessage at the start of each new revert attempt
  in RemediationHistoryPanel
- Replace AuthProvider  cast with a typed
  authProviderProps object so TypeScript enforces the discriminated
  union contract
- Add ErrorBoundary tests covering className wrapper presence and
  absence

* test(heureka): fix ErrorBoundary className test assertion

Replace nodeName check with querySelector("div[class]") — the Component
renders a plain <div> so container.firstChild is always a DIV regardless
of the wrapper, making the previous assertion always fail.

* fix(heureka): addresses remaining code review comments

- Move useAuthUserId() into FalsePositiveModal directly, removing the
  authUserId prop and its prop-drilling through IssuesDataRow
- Replace inline import() type expressions with properly imported
  AuthState in App.tsx (both AppProps and toEmbeddedAuth)
- Add error handling for background poll invalidateQueries failures;
  surface the error to the user via a timed message above the tabs

* fix(heureka): fetches always fresh data in RemediationHistoryPanel after mutation

* feat(heureka): use remediations as source of truth for active/remediated split

Instead of relying on the vulnerability status field (which the backend takes
5–6 minutes to update), both tabs now query all vulnerabilities and use the
remediations query to split them client-side: CVEs with a remediation record
appear in the Remediated tab, CVEs without appear in the Active tab.

This makes mark-false-positive and revert reflect immediately in the UI.
Background polling is removed as it is no longer needed.

* feat(heureka): adds tests for active/remediated vulnerability split logic

* fix(heureka): revert useAuth() in FalsePositiveModal and document React instance conflict

* fix(heureka): externalize React in greenhouse-auth-provider and use useAuth() directly

* test(heureka): wrap tests with AuthProvider after useAuth() migration

* chore(heureka): simplify AuthProvider props inline per reviewer suggestion

the bot would show the opponent's book move instead of the user's:

   only the board part. Prevents a result for white's turn from

   for the 400ms debounce. The old suggestion is removed as soon
   as the DOM changes, eliminating the stale-arrow window.

3. Reduce smart poll suppression from 1500ms to 300ms so the
   polling fallback kicks in faster if MutationObserver misses

commit.message:
fix: preserve previous CSS/JS assets across deploys for Clarity recording fidelity

Clarity session recordings render as unstyled HTML because Vite generates
new content-hashed filenames each build, and GitHub Pages replaces all files
atomically — so CSS URLs referenced in recorded sessions 404 after the next
deploy.

Solution: download live site's CSS/JS assets before build, merge them into
dist/ after build. Old assets survive alongside new ones for up to 30 days,
giving Clarity recordings time to expire naturally.

- scripts/preserve-clarity-assets.mjs: --download fetches live assets,
  --merge copies cached assets into dist/assets/
- deploy.yml: GitHub Actions cache persists assets across runs
- Both steps are continue-on-error to never block deploys

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Eliminate ALL live relay requests to remote nodes

The dashboard JS pollFlows() was making /api/relay/dandroid1/api/flow/list
calls that opened tunnel connections — separate from the backend fix.

Fixes:
1. Removed remote flow relay from pollFlows() JS — only polls local flows
2. Relay endpoint now returns cached/empty for ALL GET requests to remote
   nodes, never opening live tunnel connections. All remote data comes
   from the 30s background _remote_fetcher_loop.

The relay endpoint still allows live POST/PUT/DELETE (write operations)
since those are intentional user actions, not background polls.

Tunnel connection budget after all fixes:
- Background fetcher: 1 request per 30s (persistent connection)
- Frontend: 0 relay requests (cache only)
- User actions: on-demand only (write_to_pane, exec, etc)

commit.message:
fix: migrate to BAP/PP API scopes to resolve AADSTS65002 in corporate tenants (#11)

## Problem

The extension was requesting the
`https://service.flow.microsoft.com/user_impersonation` scope. In
corporate tenants where user consent is disabled by policy, this causes
an **AADSTS65002** preauthorization error, completely blocking sign-in
for enterprise developers.

## Solution

Switch to Microsoft first-party API scopes that are pre-authorized in
all Microsoft Entra tenants:

| Scope | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `https://api.bap.microsoft.com/.default` | Environment listing (BAP
API) |
| `https://api.powerplatform.com/.default` | Cloud flows & flow runs
(Power Platform API) |

This is the same pattern used by Microsoft's own [Power Platform Tools
VS Code
extension](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=microsoft-IsvExpTools.powerplatform-vscode).
These scopes do not require admin consent or app registration.

## Changes

### `src/auth/AuthProvider.ts`
- Removes the single `service.flow.microsoft.com` scope
- Adds `getBapToken()` and `getPpApiToken()` — separate tokens for the
two APIs
- Maps sovereign cloud API base URLs to their corresponding BAP/PP API
scopes
- Exposes `bapBaseUrl` and `ppApiBaseUrl` getters for the client to use
- `signIn()`: fixed `forceNewSession` → `createIfNone` (avoids forcing
re-auth when already signed in)

### `src/api/PowerAutomateClient.ts`
- `listEnvironments`: migrated to BAP API (`api.bap.microsoft.com`)
- `listFlows`, `getFlow`, `listFlowRuns`: migrated to Power Platform API
(`api.powerplatform.com`)
- Methods not yet supported by the PP API now throw a clear "not yet
available" error (handled by the `registerTools.ts` catch wrapper —
Copilot Chat will see a user-friendly message)

### `package.json`
- Default `apiBaseUrl` updated to `https://api.powerplatform.com`
- Sovereign cloud enum values updated to PP API URLs

### `docs/azure-app-registration.md` & `README.md`
- Docs rewritten to reflect no app registration requirement
- Removes stale `service.flow.microsoft.com` references

## Temporarily reduced API surface

Some API methods now throw "not yet available" until the Power Platform
API achieves parity with the legacy `service.flow.microsoft.com`
endpoints. These are clearly documented in the client. Affected
operations:
- `listConnections`, `createOrUpdateFlow`, `setFlowState`,
`addFlowToSolution`
- `getFlowRunActions`, `getFlowRunActionInputsOutputs`
- `resubmitFlowRun`, `cancelFlowRun`, `getFlowHttpSchema`,
`getFlowTriggerUrl`, `triggerFlow`

**Working:** `listEnvironments`, `listFlows`, `getFlow`, `listFlowRuns`

## Supersedes

Closes and supersedes #10 — this PR solves the same corporate-tenant
auth problem at the root cause (wrong scope) rather than adding an Azure
CLI workaround.

## Testing

- [ ] Sign in with a personal Microsoft account
- [ ] Sign in with a corporate account in a tenant with user consent
disabled
- [ ] Environments list loads correctly
- [ ] Flows list loads for an environment
- [ ] Flow runs load for a selected flow
- [ ] Unavailable tools return a clear error message in Copilot Chat

---------

commit.message:
fix(task-116): force-push repair branch on reruns; fix clipped_text edge case (#147)

Two edge cases surfaced during the live TASK-116 repair-lane smoke tests
that the earlier follow-up commits didn't fully close.

## Changes

- **Force-push repair branch on reruns** — `cmd_finalize` now uses `git
push --force -u origin` instead of a plain push. When a run successfully
pushes the repair branch but then fails to create the PR (as happened in
run `24164768702`), a subsequent forced rerun rebuilds the local branch
from `FETCH_HEAD` and the plain push is rejected because the remote
already has diverged history. Repair branches are automation-owned so
force push is safe.

  ```python
  # before
  git("push", "-u", "origin", replacement_branch)
  # after
  git("push", "--force", "-u", "origin", replacement_branch)
  ```

- **`clipped_text` edge case** — when `limit <= len(ellipsis)`, now
returns `normalized[:limit]` (plain truncation) instead of
`ellipsis[:limit]`, which would emit a meaningless `".."` or `"."`.
Unreachable with the current `MAX_REPLACEMENT_SUMMARY_CHARS = 2500`
caller, but the guard is now semantically correct.

  ```python
  # before
  if limit <= len(ellipsis):
      return ellipsis[:limit]   # e.g. ".." or "." — confusing
  # after
  if limit <= len(ellipsis):
      return normalized[:limit]  # plain truncation
  ```

Fix ConfigurationBinder failing to bind empty array to constructor parameter (#126470)

Fix #126312

When binding an empty JSON array (`[]`) to a constructor parameter of
type `string[]`, `ConfigurationBinder` threw:

```
ArgumentException: Object of type 'System.String' cannot be converted to type 'System.String[]'.
```

## Root Cause

In `BindParameter`, the `BindingPoint` was initialized with
`config.GetSection(parameterName).Value` — the config section's string
value. For an empty JSON array `[]`, this value is `""` (empty string).
This caused `BindInstance` to skip the empty-collection creation code
path, which requires `bindingPoint.Value` to be `null`. The empty string
value was then returned as the parameter value, causing the
`ArgumentException` when the constructor was invoked via reflection.

For comparison, property binding correctly initializes the
`BindingPoint` with `property.GetValue(instance)` (typically `null` for
uninitialized arrays), so the empty-collection path works.

## Fix

Remove the `initialValue` argument from the `BindingPoint` constructor
in `BindParameter`, so it defaults to `null`. This is correct because:

- Constructor parameters have no "existing value" to preserve
- The config section's value is already read inside `BindInstance` from
the `config` parameter directly — passing it as `initialValue` was
redundant
- For simple types (string, int, etc.), `TryConvertValue` succeeds and
overwrites the initial value regardless

## Test

Added `TestBindingEmptyArrayToConstructorParameter` — reproduces the
exact scenario from the issue (empty JSON array bound to a `string[]`
constructor parameter with a null default).
